proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features, feature_023=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than feature_023=0 patients.
Among the binary features, feature_011=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than feature_011=0 patients.
Among the binary features, feature_016=1 patients have longer pfs_months than feature_016=0 patients.
Among the binary features, feature_018=1 patients have longer pfs_months than feature_018=0 patients.
feature_028=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than feature_028=0 patients.
feature_031=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than feature_031=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: 2.92 mo (feature_023=1, n=32365) vs 4.36 mo (feature_023=0, n=17635); diff -1.44 mo, Welch t=-71.1, p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (smoking status: current/former/never) is associated with pfs_months, with current smokers showing shorter PFS than never smokers.
feature_006=squamous patients have shorter pfs_months than feature_006=adenocarcinoma patients.
feature_014 has a monotonic negative association with pfs_months across its three ordered levels (0, 1, 2).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: former=3.61, current=2.99, never=3.67. One-way ANOVA F-test p=3.7e-187. Current smokers have the shortest PFS; never and former are similar.
Mean PFS: squamous=2.81 mo (n=13901) vs adenocarcinoma=3.67 mo (n=36099); diff -0.86 mo, p<1e-300.

Mean PFS by feature_014: 0->4.33 (n=17557), 1->3.19 (n=24962), 2->2.15 (n=7481). Strict monotonic decrease across levels; F-test p<1e-300. Likely an ordered severity score (e.g., ECOG performance status).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_017 (continuous, 0-28) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_025 (continuous, 1.7-5.5) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
Most other continuous laboratory-like features (feature_019, 024, 010, 003, 030, 008, 004, 013, 026, 029, 007, 033, 009) have weak or null univariate correlations with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.757 (p<1e-300). feature_015 is by far the strongest single predictor of pfs_months in the cohort.
Pearson r=-0.127 (p=7.5e-179). Higher feature_017 is associated with shorter PFS.
Pearson r=+0.099 (p=2.8e-109). Weak but significant positive correlation.

analyses.code: for c in float_cols: stats.pearsonr(df[c], df['pfs_months'])

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS adjusting for all features simultaneously, feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_016, feature_018, hist_squamous, smoke_current, feature_017, feature_025, feature_005, feature_031, feature_032 retain independent associations with pfs_months and explain a large share of the variance.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2 = 0.894. Independent (adjusted) effects: feature_015 +0.168/unit (p<1e-300), feature_014 -1.10/level (p<1e-300), feature_023 -1.42 (p<1e-300), feature_011 -0.91 (p<1e-300), feature_016 +0.89 (p<1e-300), feature_018 +0.32 (p<1e-300), hist_squamous -0.72 (p<1e-300), smoke_current -0.55 (p<1e-300), feature_017 -0.073/unit (p<1e-300), feature_025 +0.46/unit (p<1e-300), feature_005 -0.11 (p=7e-9), feature_031 -0.20 (p<1e-300), feature_032 -0.0004/unit (p=1.5e-28). All other features non-significant (p>0.05) after adjustment, including feature_022, feature_028, feature_002, feature_021, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027, smoke_former, and the 13 weak labs from h3_4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between feature_016 and feature_018 on pfs_months: PFS in patients with both equal to 1 is greater than the sum of the two main effects predicts.
There is a negative interaction between feature_016 and feature_031 on pfs_months: feature_016=1 helps only when feature_031=0.

analyses.result_summary: Two-way interaction model: beta(feature_016)=-0.121, beta(feature_018)=-0.005, beta(feature_016*feature_018)=+2.558 (p<1e-300). The synergy is enormous: PFS is virtually unchanged unless both equal 1.

Two-way interaction model: beta(feature_016)=+1.542, beta(feature_031)=+0.011, beta(feature_016*feature_031)=-1.694 (p=3.2e-178). When feature_031=1, the feature_016 benefit is wiped out. feature_031 acts as a near-complete suppressor of the feature_016 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all 13 binary features treated as candidate treatments, feature_018 shows the largest treatment-effect heterogeneity by other binary features (binary x binary interaction screen).
feature_023, with its large adjusted main effect, shows clinically modest heterogeneity by other binary features (interaction beta < 0.2 for all binary modifiers).

analyses.code: for tx,mod in product(candidate_tx, all_features): fit OLS PFS ~ tx + mod + tx:mod
analyses.result_summary: Within an interaction screen of every binary candidate against every other feature, the largest interaction term (in magnitude and significance) is feature_016 x feature_018 (beta=+2.56, p<1e-300), followed by feature_016 x feature_031 (beta=-1.69, p=3.2e-178). feature_028 x smoke_current (-1.05, p=5e-17) and feature_028 x feature_016 (-1.21, p=3.5e-16) are also significant. No comparable synergy is seen for any other binary.

analyses.code: for mod in all_features: fit OLS PFS ~ feature_023 + mod + feature_023:mod
analyses.result_summary: Largest binary interactions for feature_023 are with feature_014 (beta=+0.110, p=6e-5), feature_012 (+0.108, p=0.007), feature_002 (+0.130, p=0.020), feature_028 (+0.192, p=0.035), feature_009 (+0.080, p=0.043). All are quantitatively small and the bulk of feature_023's effect (beta_main = -1.42) is uniform across subgroups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the 2x2 cross-tabulation of feature_016 by feature_018, the cell (feature_016=1, feature_018=1) has substantially higher mean pfs_months than the other three cells, which are roughly equal.

analyses.result_summary: Cell means (PFS mo): (f016=0,f018=0)=3.334 (n=28334), (f016=0,f018=1)=3.329 (n=15298), (f016=1,f018=0)=3.213 (n=4122), (f016=1,f018=1)=5.766 (n=2246). The double-positive cell exceeds the other three by ~2.5 months; the other three are statistically indistinguishable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_016=0 patients, feature_018=1 has no effect on pfs_months.
Within feature_016=1 patients, feature_018=1 has a large positive effect on pfs_months.
Within feature_016=1, the feature_018 effect on PFS is concentrated in feature_031=0 patients and is null in feature_031=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1: feature_018 effect when feature_031=0: PFS 7.876 (n=1237, f018=1) vs 3.232 (n=2271, f018=0); diff +4.644 mo, t=53.4, p<1e-300. When feature_031=1: PFS 3.179 (n=1009) vs 3.191 (n=1851); diff -0.012 mo, t=-0.15, p=0.88. Three-way interaction confirmed: feature_018 helps only the (feature_016=1, feature_031=0) subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple-positive subgroup defined by feature_016=1 AND feature_018=1 AND feature_031=0 has mean pfs_months approximately 4.6 months higher than the rest of the cohort.
Outside the triple-positive subgroup, feature_016, feature_018, and feature_031 each have approximately null effects on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Triple-positive (n=1237): mean PFS = 7.876 mo (median 8.03), SD 2.64. Rest (n=48763): mean PFS = 3.319 mo (median 3.25), SD 2.10. Unadjusted difference = +4.557 mo.

analyses.code: for c in ['feature_016','feature_018','feature_031']: stats.ttest_ind on df[df.triple==0]
analyses.result_summary: After excluding the triple-positive subgroup, feature_016 diff=-0.126 (p=5e-5), feature_018 diff=+0.001 (p=0.97), feature_031 diff=-0.000 (p=0.99). All near-null; the entire signal of the three features lives inside the triple-positive cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adjusting for all 33 baseline covariates plus the three component flags, the triple-positive indicator (feature_016=1 AND feature_018=1 AND feature_031=0) retains a large independent positive association with pfs_months (beta > 4 mo, p<1e-300).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted multivariable OLS (R^2=0.946): beta(triple)=+4.59 mo (p<1e-300); beta(feature_016)=+0.002 (p=0.78), beta(feature_018)=+0.002 (p=0.68), beta(feature_031)=+0.001 (p=0.83) - the three component main effects collapse to zero once the interaction term is included. The triple-positive subgroup is the entire signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_016=1 stratum, the feature_018 x feature_031 interaction term in an adjusted OLS model approximately equals -1 times the feature_018 main effect, i.e. the feature_018 benefit is fully abolished by feature_031=1.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1 (n=6368), adjusted: beta(feature_018)=+4.591 (p<1e-300), beta(feature_031)=-0.001 (p=0.95), beta(feature_018*feature_031)=-4.585 (p<1e-300). Cancellation is essentially complete - the interaction term absorbs the full main effect when feature_031=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup (feature_016=1, feature_018=1, feature_031=0; n=1237), feature_028=1 patients have substantially shorter PFS than feature_028=0 patients - i.e., feature_028 acts as an additional suppressor of the response.
Within the triple-positive subgroup, feature_005=1 patients have substantially shorter PFS than feature_005=0 patients - feature_005 is another additional suppressor.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on subset df[df.triple==1] split by feature_028
analyses.result_summary: Within triple-positive: feature_028=1 PFS=2.354 (n=46) vs feature_028=0 PFS=8.089 (n=1191); diff -5.735 mo (p=7.2e-22). The 46 triple-positive feature_028=1 patients lose the entire response. Note feature_028's univariate main effect on the full cohort is only -0.81 mo, so the within-subgroup effect is ~7x larger.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on subset df[df.triple==1] split by feature_005
analyses.result_summary: Within triple-positive: feature_005=1 PFS=3.411 (n=39) vs feature_005=0 PFS=8.021 (n=1198); diff -4.610 mo (p=2.4e-14). feature_005's univariate main effect is only -0.12 mo, so within-subgroup it is ~38x larger - clear suppressor of the response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features tested as effect modifiers within feature_016=1, only feature_031, feature_028, and feature_005 substantially abrogate the feature_018 benefit; feature_011, feature_022, feature_023, feature_002, smoking, and histology produce only modest changes consistent with their additive main effects.

analyses.code: Within f016=1 stratum, compute (PFS|f018=1) - (PFS|f018=0) when each modifier=0 vs =1.
analyses.result_summary: Effect of feature_018 within f016=1, by modifier (effect when modifier=0 vs =1; signed difference = suppression magnitude): feature_031 +4.64 vs -0.01 (suppression 4.65); feature_028 +2.67 vs -0.35 (3.02); feature_005 +2.63 vs +0.08 (2.55); feature_022 +2.58 vs +2.12 (0.46); feature_011 +2.63 vs +2.33 (0.31); feature_002 +2.59 vs +2.33 (0.26); smoke_former +2.44 vs +2.66 (-0.22); feature_012 +2.66 vs +2.44 (0.22); smoke_current +2.62 vs +2.47 (0.15); hist_squamous +2.62 vs +2.50 (0.11); feature_023 +2.61 vs +2.51 (0.10); feature_027 +2.52 vs +2.62 (-0.10); feature_021 +2.57 vs +2.51 (0.06); feature_020 +2.55 vs +2.55 (0.01). Only feature_031, feature_028, and feature_005 produce >1-month suppression.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The fully refined responder subgroup defined by feature_016=1 AND feature_018=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0 has mean PFS approximately 8.2 months and is approximately 5 months greater than the rest of the cohort, larger than the unrefined triple subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Quintuple-condition responder subgroup (n=1154): mean PFS = 8.234 mo (median 8.21), SD 2.27. Rest (n=48846): mean PFS = 3.318 mo (median 3.25), SD 2.10. Unadjusted difference = +4.916 mo (vs the unrefined triple's +4.557 mo).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After full multivariable adjustment for all 33 covariates including the five component flags, the quintuple responder indicator retains a large independent association with pfs_months (beta ~ +5 mo, p<1e-300) and pushes model R^2 to ~0.98.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS R^2 = 0.979. Beta(quint)=+4.923 mo (p<1e-300). Component main effects shrink to near-zero once the indicator is included, confirming that the quintuple subgroup definition is the locus of essentially all of the feature_016/feature_018/feature_031/feature_028/feature_005 signal on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the (feature_016=1, feature_031=0) responder pool, feature_015 (range 30-90) modifies the magnitude of the feature_018 effect: older patients gain even more from feature_018 than younger patients (positive feature_018 x feature_015 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Within (feature_016=1, feature_031=0) (n=3508): beta(feature_018)=+2.566 mo, beta(feature_015)=+0.167/unit, beta(feature_018 x feature_015)=+0.030/unit (p=2.0e-9). Mean feature_018 effect at the cohort mean of feature_015 (~65) is approximately 2.57 + 0.030*65 = 4.5 mo, rising to ~5.3 mo at feature_015=90 and falling to ~3.6 mo at feature_015=30. Direction: positive interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive responder cell, feature_017 retains the same negative association with PFS that it shows in the full cohort, confirming feature_017 acts as a continuous prognostic factor independent of the responder structure.
Most of the other lab-like continuous features (feature_004, 008, 010, 013, 026, 029, 030, 032, 033, 003, 007, 009) remain non-significant within the responder pool.

analyses.result_summary: Within triple-positive (n=1237): feature_017 r = -0.096 (p=7.2e-4). Magnitude similar to its full-cohort effect (r=-0.127). feature_017 functions as a uniform negative prognostic.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr within triple-positive for each continuous
analyses.result_summary: Within triple-positive (n=1237): feature_010 r=+0.061 (p=0.033), feature_013 r=-0.051 (p=0.072), feature_033 r=-0.047 (p=0.096); all other continuous labs are non-significant (p>0.1). No new heterogeneity from continuous variables beyond feature_015 and feature_017.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023, despite its large adjusted main effect (-1.42), shows no concentrated treatment-effect heterogeneity comparable to feature_018; its largest binary modifier (feature_014) only changes the effect by ~0.11 per level - it acts mostly as a uniform prognostic factor.

analyses.code: for mod in all_features: PFS ~ feature_023 + mod + feature_023:mod
analyses.result_summary: Largest interactions for feature_023: with feature_015 (continuous, beta=-0.013/unit, p=2e-25 - scale issue, magnitude small per patient); with feature_014 (beta=+0.110, p=6e-5); with feature_012 (+0.108, p=0.007); with feature_002 (+0.130, p=0.020). All small in magnitude relative to the -1.42 main effect. feature_023's effect is broadly homogeneous: it does not have a 'responder subgroup' signature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011, similarly, shows no concentrated treatment-effect heterogeneity by other binary features comparable to feature_018; the largest interactions are small modifications of an essentially homogeneous main effect.

analyses.code: for mod in all_features: PFS ~ feature_011 + mod + feature_011:mod
analyses.result_summary: Largest interactions for feature_011: feature_015 (continuous beta=-0.011/unit, p=5e-15), hist_squamous (+0.155, p=0.002), feature_014 (+0.089, p=0.005), feature_002 (+0.169, p=0.008). Modest, no concentrated subgroup. feature_011's effect is broadly uniform.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive three-way binary subgroup search across all triples of binary features identifies (feature_016=1, feature_018=1, feature_031=0) as the unique cell with the largest within-vs-out PFS differential (~+4.7 mo); no other binary triple comes close.

analyses.code: for (a,b,c) in combinations(binary, 3): groupby and find largest within-vs-rest diff with n_in>=50
analyses.result_summary: Top 5 three-way binary subgroups by within-vs-rest PFS differential: (feature_016=1,feature_018=1,feature_031=0) +4.56 mo (n=1237); (feature_023=0,feature_016=1,feature_018=1) +3.33 mo (n=807, dominated by the f016*f018 synergy); (feature_016=1,feature_028=0,feature_018=1) +2.59 mo (n=2159, mirror of the suppressor finding); (feature_023=0,feature_028=0,smoke_current=0) +1.53 mo (n=11621, additive prognostic stack); (feature_016=1,feature_018=1,hist_squamous=0) +2.76 mo (n=1449). The top result is the f016*f018 synergy with f031 as suppressor; everything else either rephrases that interaction or is a small additive stack of main effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016=1 status is approximately balanced across other features (it functions like a randomized biomarker assignment): no covariate strongly predicts feature_016=1 in a logistic model.
feature_018=1 status is also broadly balanced; its allocation is not strongly determined by other features, consistent with treatment-like assignment.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression for feature_016=1: smallest p-values are feature_002 (beta=-0.080, p=0.041), feature_009 (-0.054, p=0.046), feature_003 (+0.015, p=0.047). All effects are small and most are non-significant. No strong baseline predictor of feature_016=1.

Logistic regression for feature_018=1: smallest p-values are feature_025 (-0.062, p=0.001), feature_010 (-0.013, p=0.005), feature_032 (+0.0002, p=0.036), feature_019 (+0.092, p=0.037). Modest. Feature_018 is mildly predicted by a few labs, but no strong assignment confounding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 is jointly a strong prognostic and a continuous effect modifier: it shows a positive Pearson correlation with PFS in every subgroup we examine, and additionally amplifies the feature_018 treatment effect within (feature_016=1, feature_031=0).

analyses.code: Pearson r within: full cohort, triple-positive subset, feature_016=0 subset
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r(feature_015, PFS): full cohort 0.757; triple-positive (n=1237) 0.759; feature_016=0 (n=43632) ~0.76 by inspection of multivariable beta. The feature_015-PFS slope is essentially identical across subgroups (no scale-shift), but the absolute effect size of feature_018 within the responder pool grows with feature_015 (h17 interaction beta=+0.030/unit). Hence feature_015 is both a uniform prognostic and a multiplicative modifier of treatment magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After collapsing the responder structure into the single 'quint' indicator, a parsimonious final model PFS ~ feature_015 + feature_014 + feature_023 + feature_011 + hist_squamous + smoke_current + feature_017 + feature_025 + feature_032 + quint achieves R^2 close to the full saturated model, confirming we have captured the principal signals.

analyses.result_summary: Parsimonious OLS R^2 = 0.978 (vs full-saturated 0.979). Coefficients (all p<1e-30): feature_015 +0.167/unit, feature_014 -1.105/level, feature_023 -1.418, feature_011 -0.910, hist_squamous -0.359, smoke_current -0.276, feature_017 -0.072/unit, feature_025 +0.464/unit, feature_032 -0.0005/unit, quint +4.92 (anchor of the heterogeneity signal). The 10-variable model captures essentially the entire PFS signal in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: Treating feature_018 as the candidate treatment for pfs_months, the treatment effect is concentrated in - and approximately exclusive to - the subgroup defined by feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0. In that subgroup the feature_018 effect on PFS is approximately +4.5 to +5 months (positive, meaning longer PFS); outside that subgroup the feature_018 effect is approximately zero. Within the responder subgroup, the magnitude of the feature_018 benefit additionally scales positively with feature_015.

Equivalently: among feature_016=1 patients, feature_031, feature_028, and feature_005 each act as near-complete suppressors of the feature_018 benefit. feature_031 (prevalence 45%) suppresses ~100% of the effect; feature_028 (prevalence 5%) and feature_005 (prevalence 3%) each abolish it within their small strata.

analyses.code: Within (f016=1, f031=0, f028=0, f005=0), test feature_018 effect; outside this stratum, test feature_018 effect.
analyses.result_summary: Within the (feature_016=1, feature_031=0, feature_028=0, feature_005=0) stratum: feature_018=1 PFS = ~8.2 mo (n=1154) vs feature_018=0 PFS = ~3.2 mo (matched stratum, n~2179) - difference approximately +5.0 mo (p<1e-200). Outside this stratum, feature_018 effect on PFS is essentially zero (diff ~0.001 mo, p>0.9). The adjusted full-model 'quint' indicator beta = +4.92 mo (p<1e-300, R^2 contribution that lifts the model from 0.894 to 0.979). Suppression by feature_031: within feature_016=1, feature_018 effect drops from +4.64 mo (when feature_031=0) to -0.01 mo (when feature_031=1), p_interaction<1e-300. Suppression by feature_028 and feature_005: within the (feature_016=1, feature_018=1, feature_031=0) responder cell, feature_028=1 reduces PFS by 5.74 mo (p=7e-22) and feature_005=1 reduces PFS by 4.61 mo (p=2e-14), each near-fully cancelling the response. Direction signed positive on PFS (longer = better) for feature_018 within the defined responder subgroup.
